bottom_line: CR-00086 is clean — one MED finding about pre-existing integration-test failures causing false QV gate failures (e.g., test_every_cli_command_documented_in_spec blocking items that add new CLI commands). No fix-cycle waste, no environment thrash, no prompt gaps.
coverage_notes: Full read of all non-empty run logs (S01-S08, S09-S15, S16). S05 run1 was empty (0 bytes); S05 run2 was the actual run. S12 unit log: sampled head + tail. S13 run1: grep-extracted error lines; S13 run4: results summary + tail. SKILL.md diff confirmed byte-identical. Workflow file confirmed workflow_dispatch present. All 4 test files verified per-metric placeholder coverage. DB signal confirmed UP.

findings.title: Pre-existing integration-test failures cause false QV gate failures
findings.recommendation: Add a pytest deselect mechanism (via Makefile --deselect or pyproject.ini_options.deselect) for the three known-failing tests so they don't block sound CRs: test_every_cli_command_documented_in_spec (fails whenever a new CLI command is added before the spec lag is caught), test_i_00063_apply_succeeds_when_no_blocking_lock and test_i_00063_apply_does_not_self_deadlock_when_caller_holds_share_lock (daemon/migration pre-existing failures), and test_semgrep_baseline_is_zero_blocking_findings (SAST baseline drift). An alternative is to add a KNOWN_TEST_DRIFT convention to the QV gate process.
evidence: ai-dev/logs/CR-00086_S13_run4.log:7096 — = 3335 passed, 29 skipped, 5 xfailed, 2 xpassed, 232 warnings in 303.22s (pass after retry; failures in run1 were pre-existing)
findings.paste_prompt: /iw-new-cr Add pytest --deselect guards for three known-pre-existing integration test failures (test_cli_spec_conformance, test_phase2_apply_no_self_deadlock variants, test_semgrep_baseline_is_zero_blocking_findings) so they do not cause false QV gate failures for CRs that add new CLI commands or update SAST baselines. Analyzed in CR-00086 (see ai-dev/work/CR-00086/reports/CR-00086_self_assess_report.md). Target: Makefile or pyproject.toml. Effort: S.
